response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but the profile misses every mandatory pillar of this role: C++ exposure amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a thesis project, far short of the required 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++; SQL experience lists MySQL and MongoDB rather than SQL Server or Oracle; and there is no fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain background — RIDE Capital was consumer-facing fintech, not capital markets. The low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby and solid CI/CD craftsmanship are genuine assets, but they do not substitute for the core C++/finance requirements of a consulting engagement expected to deliver on critical trading systems from day one.
strengths: Long, progressive senior/staff-level engineering career with broad polyglot experience
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, DevOps tooling, and testing expertise
concerns: C++ experience is brief (~7 months professional) and roughly a decade old, versus 5-10 years required
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MySQL/MongoDB
No fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain knowledge; no Summit platform exposure

key_factors.factor: Missing capital markets / fixed income domain
Strong general engineering and performance-systems background
